label: Slack
description: Read and send messages in Slack channels and DMs using a bot installed in your workspace.

form.title: Connect Slack
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and post messages in your Slack workspace.

methods.label: Bot User OAuth Token
methods.description: Paste an xoxb- token from a Slack app installed in your workspace. Simplest path — no OAuth dance required, just create an app, add scopes, install it, and copy the token.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Slack App and get a Bot Token

This is the fastest way to connect Anton to Slack. You create a Slack app, grant it the permissions Anton needs, install it to your workspace, and paste the token.

### Steps

1. Go to [api.slack.com/apps](https://api.slack.com/apps) and click **Create New App → From scratch**.
2. Give it a name (e.g. `Anton`) and pick the workspace you want to connect.
3. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth & Permissions**.
4. Scroll to **Scopes → Bot Token Scopes** and add the scopes Anton needs, for example:
   - `channels:history` — read messages in public channels
   - `channels:read` — list public channels
   - `chat:write` — post messages
   - `im:history` / `im:read` — read direct messages
   - `groups:history` / `groups:read` — read private channels the bot is invited to
5. Scroll back up and click **Install to Workspace**, then click **Allow**.
6. After installation, the page shows a **Bot User OAuth Token** starting with `xoxb-`. Copy it.
7. Paste the token into the **Bot Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token is only shown once per install. If you lose it, reinstall the app from the same page to generate a new one.
- The bot must be invited to any private channel it should read (type `/invite @YourAppName` in the channel).
- Tokens are workspace-scoped — repeat this process for each additional workspace.

fields.name: bot_token
fields.label: Bot Token
fields.description: Bot User OAuth Token from api.slack.com/apps → your app → OAuth & Permissions. Starts with xoxb-.

fields.name: default_channel
fields.label: Default Channel
fields.description: Optional. The channel Anton posts to when no channel is specified (e.g. #general or a channel ID).

methods.label: Sign in with Slack (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Use your own Slack app's client credentials to run a full OAuth 2.0 flow. More setup required — best for multi-workspace or automated scenarios.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with your own Slack App

This flow opens a browser window so you can authorize Anton via Slack's consent screen. You must first create a Slack app and supply its client credentials.

### Steps

1. Go to [api.slack.com/apps](https://api.slack.com/apps) and click **Create New App → From scratch**.
2. Name it (e.g. `Anton`) and pick your workspace.
3. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth & Permissions**.
4. Under **Redirect URLs**, add `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect to capture the code).
5. Scroll to **Scopes → Bot Token Scopes** and add the scopes you need (e.g. `channels:history`, `chat:write`, `channels:read`).
6. In the left sidebar, click **Basic Information**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the **App Credentials** section.
7. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**. A browser window will open Slack's authorization screen.
8. Click **Allow**. Anton captures the token automatically.

### Notes

- If you see a redirect mismatch error, double-check that `http://127.0.0.1` is listed under Redirect URLs exactly.
- Slack's OAuth 2.0 v2 returns a bot token (`xoxb-`) alongside the access response — Anton stores this for API calls.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From api.slack.com/apps → your app → Basic Information → App Credentials.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Found in the same App Credentials section.

fields.name: default_channel
fields.label: Default Channel
fields.description: Optional. The channel Anton posts to when no channel is specified.
